title: Solver: warm-start convergence robustness improvements
analysis: The task has 3 items from task-495 review suggestions. Analysis of main reveals items 2 (dimension-scaling FEASIBLE_OPT_ITERS) and 3 (fallback on drift) are ALREADY IMPLEMENTED on main with full test coverage: FEASIBLE_OPT_ITERS_PER_DIM=500 scaled as (n_params+1).min(MAX_ITERS), initial_fallback_values logic, and 4 integration tests (warm_start_scales_iterations_with_dimension, warm_start_falls_back_to_initial_when_optimizer_drifts_infeasible, warm_start_optimizes_when_possible, infeasible_initial_not_rescued_by_fallback). Item 1 (convergence reporting) is NOT implemented — a NOTE comment at line 639-643 acknowledges the gap. TerminationReason from argmin is never extracted from the solver result. The approach: extract TerminationReason via result.state().get_termination_reason(), add tracing logging for convergence quality and fallback events. SolveResult::Solved will NOT be modified (70+ pattern matches across 13 files in 6+ crates would need updating). The tracing crate is already a workspace dependency (version 0.1) used by reify-runtime. Adding it to reify-constraints is consistent. A new integration test will verify the warm-start convergence-budget-exhaustion scenario returns Solved when feasible (distinct from existing tests which test sufficient budget or drift-to-infeasible).

prerequisites.description: Add tracing workspace dependency to crates/reify-constraints/Cargo.toml [dependencies] section

steps.description: Write failing integration test `warm_start_budget_exhaustion_stays_feasible`: 2-param problem with tight constraints (each param 10mm-12mm window), wide bounds (0-100mm), both params start feasible at 11mm, minimize(p0+p1) objective. The solver should return Solved with all constraints satisfied even if the optimizer doesn't fully converge within the scaled budget. This tests the convergence-without-full-optimality scenario that item 1 addresses.

Verify step-1 test passes with existing solver code (it should pass since the behavior is already correct — constraints satisfied means Solved). If it passes, the test serves as a regression guard for the convergence reporting changes.

Write unit test `termination_reason_extracted_without_panic`: Construct a minimal 1-param problem, run the solver, and verify it returns Solved or Infeasible (not NoProgress) — confirming that TerminationReason extraction doesn't introduce panics or regressions. This test will be in solver.rs mod tests.

In solver.rs: (1) Add `use argmin::core::{State, TerminationReason}` imports. (2) After executor.run() succeeds, extract TerminationReason via `result.state().get_termination_reason()`. (3) Add `tracing::debug!` logging the termination reason, number of params, iteration budget, and whether objective was present. (4) When TerminationReason is MaxItersReached AND an objective was present, add `tracing::debug!` noting the solution satisfies constraints but objective may be suboptimal. (5) On the fallback-to-initial path (line 602-603), add `tracing::debug!` noting that optimizer drifted infeasible and falling back to initial feasible point.

Write integration test `warm_start_feasible_no_objective_early_exit`: 3-param problem where all params start feasible (centered in constraint window) with NO objective. Verify solver returns Solved immediately (early-exit path at line 496) with the initial values unchanged. This validates the pure-feasibility early-exit doesn't regress with new tracing code.
Add tracing::debug! to the early-exit path (line 496-508) logging that initial point is already feasible and no optimization needed. Verify all existing tests still pass.
Run full test suite for reify-constraints crate: `cargo test -p reify-constraints`. Verify all 18+ existing integration tests plus 3 new tests pass. Verify no regressions in unit tests.

Update the NOTE comment at line 639-643 to reflect the new TerminationReason extraction and tracing. Document that convergence quality is now logged via tracing but NOT propagated through the SolveResult API to preserve backward compatibility across 6+ consumer crates.

design_decisions.decision: Use tracing logging instead of modifying SolveResult::Solved to add convergence metadata
design_decisions.rationale: SolveResult::Solved { values } is pattern-matched in 70+ locations across 13 files in 6+ crates (reify-types, reify-eval, reify-runtime, reify-test-support, reify-constraints). Adding a field would require updating all pattern matches to use `..` syntax. The tracing approach provides observability (operators can enable debug logging to see convergence quality) without any API breaking changes. The SolveResult docstring already documents that Solved doesn't guarantee objective optimality.

design_decisions.decision: Log at debug level (not warn) for MaxItersReached with objective
design_decisions.rationale: MaxItersReached with a feasible result is a NORMAL outcome for penalty-based Nelder-Mead, especially on problems near constraint boundaries. A warn! would fire frequently in production and create noise. debug! provides the information for operators who opt in via RUST_LOG=reify_constraints=debug without alarming normal users. The SolveResult docstring serves as the user-facing indication.

design_decisions.decision: Items 2 and 3 are already implemented — plan focuses on item 1 only
design_decisions.rationale: Code on main already has: FEASIBLE_OPT_ITERS_PER_DIM=500 with (n_params+1).min(MAX_ITERS) scaling (item 2), initial_fallback_values logic with initially_feasible gating (item 3), and 4 integration tests covering both features. These were implemented in a prior task run. The remaining work is item 1 (convergence reporting via TerminationReason extraction and tracing).

design_decisions.decision: Extract TerminationReason from argmin State trait rather than from TerminationStatus enum directly
design_decisions.rationale: argmin 0.10's State trait provides get_termination_reason() -> Option<&TerminationReason> which cleanly returns None when still running or Some(reason) when terminated. This is more ergonomic than matching on TerminationStatus::Terminated(reason) manually.

reuse.how: Add tracing.workspace = true to crates/reify-constraints/Cargo.toml, reusing the workspace-level tracing = "0.1" definition already used by reify-runtime
Call result.state().get_termination_reason() after executor.run() to get Option<&TerminationReason> — same pattern as the existing result.state().get_best_param() call at line 573
Use existing vcid(), value_ref(), gt(), lt(), literal(), mm(), and() helpers for constructing test problems — same pattern as all existing integration tests

Follow the established pattern: construct ResolutionProblem with AutoParams + constraints + current_values + objective, call solver.solve(), match on SolveResult::Solved { values } and assert parameter values are within constraint bounds
